content_blocks.text:
96 
chapter 5
Let’5 build a Battleship-style 
game: “Sink a Dot Com”
It’5 you against the computer, but unlike the real 
Battleship game, in this one you don’t place any ships 
of your own. Instead, your job is to sink the computer’5 
ships in the fewest number of guesses. 
Oh, and we aren’t sinking ships. We’re killing Dot 
Coms. (Thus establishing business relevancy 50 you can 
expense the cost of this book).
Goal: Sink all of the computer’5 Dot Coms in the fewest 
number of guesses. You’re given a rating or level, based 
on how well you perform.
Setup: When the game program is launched, the 
computer places three Dot Coms on a virtual 7 x 7 
grid. When that’5 complete, the game asks for your first 
guess.
How you play: We haven’t learned to build a GUI 
yet, 50 this version works at the command-line. The 
computer will prompt you to enter a guess (a cell), that 
you’11 type at the command-line as “A3”, “C5”, etc.). 
In response to your guess, you’11 see a result at the 
command-line, either “Hit”, “Miss”, or “You sunk Pets.
com” (or whatever the lucky Dot Com of the day is). 
When you’ve sent all three Dot Coms to that big 404 in 
the sky, the game ends by printing out your rating.
7 X 7 grid
